title: Aureline service contract-state vocabulary
description: Closed contract-state vocabulary read by shell, About, service-health, CLI/headless inspect, diagnostics, and support exports. Surfaces MUST quote one of these tokens on every service-health card. Rendering an open-ended token such as "broken", "down", or "service degraded" outside this set is a contract violation. Adding a token is additive-minor and bumps the contract-state schema_version; repurposing an existing token is breaking. The set deliberately separates stale, contract_mismatch, policy_blocked, local_only, and offline semantics so a single bad probe does not collapse into one generic state.

schema_version.description: Schema version for the contract-state vocabulary. Additive-minor changes (new tokens) bump this integer; repurposing an existing token is breaking and requires a new vocabulary.

service_contract_state_token.description: Stable token quoted by every surface that paints a service-health card. The set is closed and shared across shell, CLI/headless inspect, diagnostics, and support exports so one surface cannot drift its vocabulary while another lags.

label.description: Stable user-visible label. Quoted verbatim across surfaces.

summary.description: Short, reviewable explanation of the state. Carries no raw endpoint URLs, hostnames, credentials, or implementation-only jargon.

severity.description: Severity used by the aggregator's worst-state rollup. 0 = ready, 4 = the strongest signals (contract_mismatch, unavailable).

honest_warning.description: True when the chrome MUST light a yellow chip on the card. Only `ready` is false.
